[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm deeply disappointed and angry that Erdoğan has won outright. It feels like the future is bleak for us, especially with Demirtaş still in prison and the new system concentrating so much power in one person's hands.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: The currency crisis is hitting us hard; I feel the weight of rising prices every day. Erdoğan's framing of this situation as an attack feels hollow when we're the ones suffering. My trust in the government is at an all-time low.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a deep sense of anger and frustration with the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. It reinforces my belief that our rights are not respected, and it makes me lose even more trust in the institutions meant to protect us.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: The recent local elections were a shock to the AKP, and it gives me a glimmer of hope that change is possible. However, my anger towards the government's refusal to respect the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş still weighs heavily on me.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: The annulment of the Istanbul election feels like a blatant disregard for our democratic rights, but İmamoğlu's victory gives me hope that change is possible. I feel a mix of anger towards the government and hope for the future.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has deeply affected me. It highlights the ongoing violence against women and the government's failure to protect us. I feel a mix of anger and sadness, but also a sense of urgency to fight for our rights.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is making life even harder for us, especially with the government's inadequate support for those of us struggling. I feel a mix of anger and sadness about how our rights and needs are being neglected during this crisis.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I am deeply troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a major setback for women's rights and safety, especially given the rising violence against women. My anger and sadness are overwhelming as I see our rights being disregarded.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a deep sense of anger and fear about the closure case against HDP. It feels like a direct attack on our rights and a threat to our political representation, which is incredibly disheartening.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and fear about the economic situation and the closure case against HDP. It feels like our rights and livelihoods are under attack, and it's disheartening to see the government failing us.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration after hearing about the parliament's rejection of the pension hike. Many retirees, including those who once supported the government, are being left behind, and it feels like our voices are not being heard.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm overwhelmed by the rising inflation and the government's neglect of our needs, especially for retirees. It's hard to see the suffering around me and feel hopeful for the future.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm deeply disappointed and angry that Erdoğan has won outright. It feels like the future is bleak for us, especially with Demirtaş still in prison and the new system concentrating so much power in one person's hands.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: The currency crisis is hitting us hard; I feel the weight of rising prices every day. Erdoğan's framing of this situation as an attack feels hollow when we're the ones suffering. My trust in the government is at an all-time low.

It reinforces my belief that our rights are not respected, and it makes me lose even more trust in the institutions meant to protect us.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: The recent local elections were a shock to the AKP, and it gives me a glimmer of hope that change is possible. However, my anger towards the government's refusal to respect the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş still weighs heavily on me.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: The annulment of the Istanbul election feels like a blatant disregard for our democratic rights, but İmamoğlu's victory gives me hope that change is possible. I feel a mix of anger towards the government and hope for the future.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has deeply affected me. It highlights the ongoing violence against women and the government's failure to protect us. I feel a mix of anger and sadness, but also a sense of urgency to fight for our rights.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is making life even harder for us, especially with the government's inadequate support for those of us struggling. I feel a mix of anger and sadness about how our rights and needs are being neglected during this crisis.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I am deeply troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a major setback for women's rights and safety, especially given the rising violence against women. My anger and sadness are overwhelming as I see our rights being disregarded.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a deep sense of anger and fear about the closure case against HDP. It feels like a direct attack on our rights and a threat to our political representation, which is incredibly disheartening.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and fear about the economic situation and the closure case against HDP. It feels like our rights and livelihoods are under attack, and it's disheartening to see the government failing us.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration after hearing about the parliament's rejection of the pension hike. Many retirees, including those who once supported the government, are being left behind, and it feels like our voices are not being heard.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm overwhelmed by the rising inflation and the government's neglect of our needs, especially for retirees. It's hard to see the suffering around me and feel hopeful for the future.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm outraged by İmamoğlu's sentencing; it feels like an attack on our rights and freedoms. The government's actions only deepen my distrust and anger towards them.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I feel a mix of anger and skepticism towards the government's recent announcements. While some workers may benefit from the retirement changes, it feels like a temporary fix aimed at winning votes rather than addressing our real economic struggles.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I feel deeply frustrated and angry about the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It feels like a direct attack on our political representation and rights, especially with the elections approaching.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I'm feeling overwhelmed and fearful after the devastating earthquakes. The government's response seems inadequate, and I worry about the safety of my community. The freezing of HDP's treasury aid adds to my frustration, as it feels like our political voice is being silenced at a critical time.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling an overwhelming sense of anger and fear as I witness the government's chaotic response to the earthquake. The suffering of my community is palpable, and it feels like our voices are being ignored at a critical time.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration after the government's decision to block Twitter and TikTok during the earthquake rescue efforts. It feels like they're prioritizing control over the lives of those in need, and this only adds to my distrust in them.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I feel a mix of anger and frustration after Erdoğan admitted to problems in the earthquake response. His visit and promises feel hollow, especially given the suffering of so many. The government's actions, like blocking social media, only deepen my distrust.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm filled with anger and frustration over the government's failure to ensure building safety, which has resulted in so many lives lost. The arrest of contractors feels like a hollow response to a much larger issue rooted in corruption and negligence. I can't trust this government to protect us.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I feel a mix of anger and hope as I see the opposition mayors stepping up in the earthquake response. It's frustrating to see the government struggle while the opposition shows they can actually care for the people.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope as I see the opposition mayors stepping up in the earthquake response. It's frustrating to witness the government's struggle while the opposition shows they can actually care for the people.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling frustrated and anxious about the opposition's crisis, especially with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. It worries me that this division could weaken our chances against Erdoğan in the upcoming elections.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved that the opposition has reunited and Kılıçdaroğlu is the candidate, but I still worry about how this will play out, especially with the nationalist concerns. I hope this unity can strengthen our chances against Erdoğan.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm relieved that the Constitutional Court has lifted the HDP's treasury aid freeze, as it allows us to better support Kılıçdaroğlu in the upcoming elections. However, the ongoing closure case still worries me, and I feel a mix of hope and anger about our situation.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: The pension increase feels like a desperate attempt by Erdoğan to buy votes, but it doesn't change the fact that inflation is eroding our purchasing power. I am still worried about the opposition's ability to unite and effectively challenge this government.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I feel a mix of hope and anger after the HDP/YSP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It energizes me as a Kurdish voter, but I worry about how the nationalists will frame this against him.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I feel frustrated and angry about Erdoğan's last-minute salary raise, which feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes. The HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu gives me a glimmer of hope, but I worry about the backlash from nationalists and how it will affect our rights as Kurds.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after İnce's withdrawal. It seems like a calculated move influenced by smear tactics, and I hope this won't derail our chances against Erdoğan.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I feel a strong sense of urgency to vote against Erdoğan and support Kılıçdaroğlu. The withdrawal of İnce has left me feeling frustrated, but I believe we must unite for change.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I feel a mix of anger and sadness after the first round results. Kılıçdaroğlu's underperformance is disheartening, but I still hold onto hope for the runoff. We must unite against Erdoğan and push for change.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I feel betrayed by Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn and anti-refugee rhetoric. It makes me question whether he truly represents my interests as a Kurdish voter. I still want to see Erdoğan defeated, but I'm worried about the implications of this shift.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I feel a deep sense of betrayal with Kılıçdaroğlu's recent nationalist rhetoric, especially after Oğan's endorsement of Erdoğan. It makes me anxious about the future of Kurdish rights and democracy in Turkey.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I feel deeply conflicted about Kılıçdaroğlu's recent actions, especially the Özdağ Protocol. It feels like a betrayal of our values and could harm our community's interests.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I feel torn about Kılıçdaroğlu's recent nationalist shift and the Özdağ Protocol. It's frustrating to see the opposition adopt such rhetoric, and I worry it could alienate Kurdish voters like myself.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I feel a mix of anger and sadness about the current political situation, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn. However, I still believe that voting against Erdoğan is crucial for our future. I will support Kılıçdaroğlu in the runoff, despite my concerns.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I'm devastated by the election results. Erdoğan's victory feels like a major setback for our rights and aspirations, especially after Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist pivot. I fear for the future of our community and the ongoing struggles we face.
